title: Lumen — a from-scratch path tracer
description: A physically based Monte-Carlo path tracer built from scratch in TypeScript: participating media (fog/smoke/cloud) with Henyey-Greenstein scattering and volumetric god rays, thin-film interference for soap-bubble/oil-slick iridescence, smooth triangle meshes (icosphere, lathe & OBJ import) with barycentric-interpolated normals, a SAH bounding volume hierarchy, GGX microfacet BSDFs with VNDF sampling, smooth and frosted dielectrics, spectral dispersion (prism rainbows), Beer-Lambert volumetric absorption, procedural textures, an analytic Preetham daylight sky whose sun is a sampled light, next-event estimation with multiple importance sampling, scrambled-Halton low-discrepancy sampling, adaptive variance-guided sampling with a live noise heatmap, a multi-threaded Web Worker pool, ACES tone mapping, and an edge-avoiding À-Trous denoiser — global illumination on the CPU, in your browser.
